question: What command lists all container images stored locally in the system?
context: Podman system connection list (shows all the remote Podman socket connections in the system) Podman machine list (shows all podman managed VM’s in the system) Podman version (to check the version, runs only if VM is running) Podman machine start (to start the VM) Podman info, Podman version (to check the details and if running) Podman images (lists all container images stored locally in the system) Podman pull <image-name> (Downloads an image) Podman rmi <image-id> (Removes an image) Podman search <image-name> (searches the respective image) Podman run -it -rm <image-name> (Starts a new container from the specified image, -it using this we can iterate with the container via terminal, -rm automatically removes the container when it exits keeps system clean) Podman ps (it shows all the running containers) Podman ps -a (shows all containers, including the stopped ones) Podman ps -filter status=exited (shows only exited commands) Podman run -name <container-name-that-we-can-assign> -p ext_port:int_port <container_image> (we can name the container but when working with group its better not to name since it might bring in conflicts, maps host port (ext) to container port (int) ) Podman inspect <container-name> (gives all the information about the container in JSON format) Podman port <container-name> (gives us what port is container running in) Podman rm <container-name> (to remove the specified container) Podman stop <container-name> (to stop the running container) Podman run -name <container_name_to_set> -p 8080:8080 <image_name>:<tag> (to run the image) Podman pod create -name <assign-name> (creates a new empty pod with a single infra container) Podman pod ls (it will display all the pods existing) Podman ps -a -pod (displays all the containers with their respective pod details) Podman run -dt -pod <pod_name> <container_img_name> (to run the container inside the pod, it always creates new container when we use run) Podman pod start <pod_name> (it will start the pod) Podman start <container_name> (will start the container) Podman stop <container_name> (will stop the container) Podman rm <container_name> (it will remove the container only after it stops running)

question: What is the default link to login to the SonarQube web server?

Step 4: Now using tortoise git take pull for the latest changes available in the remote repository.(i.e. Right click TortoiseGit Pull Ok) Step 5: Now using git bash run ‘git stash pop’.

answers.text: locally stores all the most recent changes in a workspace and resets the state of the workspace to the prior commit state

question: What command should you execute after 'podman -version'?
context: “podman -version” After this you can execute these commands to setup your podman environment. Open the terminal. Execute “podman machine init” to create your first machine. The init command will install a minimal installation of Fedora, customizing it to run Podman. After the init process is done, it could take few minutes. You can execute “podman machine start” to start your machine.

context: “podman -version” After this you can execute these commands to setup your podman environment. Open the terminal. Execute “podman machine init” to create your first machine. The init command will install a minimal installation of Fedora, customizing it to run Podman. After the init process is done, it could take few minutes. You can execute “podman machine start” to start your machine.

question: What version of wsl-vpnkit should I use?
context: - This kit is only required for wsl only, we won’t be needing this kit in a linux based system. To install the wsl-vpnkit you first need to download the tar.gz file. Go to this link https://github.com/sakai135/wsl-vpnkit/releases/tag/v0.4.1 and download the “wsl-vpnkit.tar.gz”. After downloading open the terminal and execute this command. “wsl -import wsl-vpnkit $env:USERPROFILE\wsl-vpnkit "<Location of your wsl-vpnkit.tar.gz >” -version 2” NOTE:
